metadata.notes: Wingspan standard overhaul: removed legacy sections format, added rules_citations with key rulings, validated content against official rulebook.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- 183 dragon cards — 148 full-grown dragons, 35 hatchlings (require caching resources and tucking cards to reach full potential)
- 75 cave cards — played to excavate spaces in your caverns before dragons can be placed
- 10 double-sided objective tiles (20 possible objectives, 4 used per game)

#### Player Components
- 1 player mat — 3 cavern rows with 4 slots each:
  - Crimson Cavern (red, top row)
  - Golden Grotto (yellow, middle row)
  - Amethyst Abyss (purple, bottom row)
- Column 1 in each cavern is pre-excavated; columns 2-4 require cave cards
- 1 adventurer meeple (moves through caverns when you explore)
- 1 guild token (tracks position on the Dragon Guild board)
- 8 player markers (for claiming guild tile benefits)

#### Resources & Tokens
- 100 resource tokens in 4 types: Meat (red), Gold (yellow), Crystals (purple), Milk (white)
- 55 egg miniatures — 1 VP each at game end, also used as escalation costs for repeated exploration and deep excavation
- 45 coin tokens — coins are NOT resources; each coin lets you take 1 action on your turn
- 20 multiplier tokens for scoring

#### Shared Components
- 1 Dragon Guild board — circular track plus 4 double-sided guild tiles (2-3 player side / 4-5 player side)
- 1 card display board (face-up dragon market)
- 1 round tracker
- 17 Automa cards (solo play)

subtopics.title: Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
1. **Player mat** — Each player takes a mat; column 1 of each cavern is pre-excavated, columns 2-4 are empty.
2. **Player pieces** — Take your adventurer meeple, guild token, and 8 player markers.
3. **Dragon deck** — Shuffle and set up the face-up display on the card display board.
4. **Cave deck** — Shuffle separately.
5. **Dragon Guild tiles** — Use the 2-3 player side or 4-5 player side based on player count.
6. **Objective tiles** — Place 4 random objective tiles face-up.

#### Player Draft
1. **Deal** — Each player receives 5 dragon cards, 3 cave cards, and 2 objective tiles.
2. **Keep dragons** — Look at your 5 dragon cards, keep 3, discard 2 face-down.
3. **Keep objective** — Look at your 2 objective tiles, keep 1, discard the other.
4. **Choose resources** — Pick 3 starting resources in any combination of meat, gold, crystals, and milk.

#### Starting State
- Player mat with column 1 pre-excavated in all 3 caverns
- 3 dragon cards and 3 cave cards in hand
- 1 secret objective tile
- 3 resources of your choice
- 0 eggs, 6 coins
- Random first player

subtopics.title: Actions & Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### How a Round Works
- Wyrmspan lasts exactly 4 rounds.
- At the start of each round, receive 6 coins plus 1 egg.
- Players take turns spending 1 coin per action until all coins are spent (or chosen to stop).

#### Your Three Actions
Every action costs 1 coin. Choose one:

1. **Excavate** — Play a cave card from your hand into the leftmost empty slot of any cavern row.
   - Column 2: 1 coin only
   - Column 3: 1 coin + 1 egg
   - Column 4: 1 coin + 2 eggs
   - The cave card's "when played" bonus activates immediately.
   - You MUST excavate a space before placing a dragon there. Column 1 is pre-excavated; columns 2-4 need cave cards.

2. **Entice Dragon** — Play a dragon card into an excavated-but-empty slot.
   - Pay the dragon's resource cost (shown on the card).
   - Place it in the leftmost excavated empty slot of the matching cavern.
   - If the dragon has a "when played" ability, resolve it now.

3. **Explore Cavern** — Your adventurer moves left-to-right through one cavern.
   - Each dragon's ability activates in sequence.
   - At the end of the row, gain the cavern's printed explore reward.
   - Escalation costs for repeated exploration of the same cavern per round: 1st time free (1 coin), 2nd time 1 coin + 1 egg, 3rd time 1 coin + 2 eggs.

#### Coins Are Your Actions
- **6 coins minimum** each round; abilities and guild bonuses can grant extra coins.
- **Extra coins are extremely valuable** — each one is an additional action.
- **Unspent coins are WASTED** — they do not carry over. Spend them all.

#### Hand Limit
- **9-item maximum** at end of turn — dragon cards + cave cards + resource tokens combined.
- If over 9, discard down to 9.

subtopics.title: The Three Caverns
subtopics.content:
#### Crimson Cavern (Red — Top Row)
- **Favors** — Meat-eating dragons.
- **Explore reward** — Resources plus Dragon Guild advancement.
- **Role** — Primary resource engine. Early dragons here mean more resources every exploration, fueling everything else.

#### Golden Grotto (Yellow — Middle Row)
- **Favors** — Gold-loving dragons.
- **Explore reward** — Dragon cards from deck or display, plus Dragon Guild advancement.
- **Role** — Card flow and tucking engine. Tucked cards = 1 VP each at game end. Dragons that tuck on exploration generate VP every time you explore.

#### Amethyst Abyss (Purple — Bottom Row)
- **Favors** — Crystal-consuming dragons.
- **Explore reward** — Cave cards plus Dragon Guild advancement.
- **Role** — Excavation pipeline. Explore to gain cave cards, then excavate more spaces. Best home for hatchling dragons — the self-sustaining card flow supports sustained feeding.

#### How Exploration Works
- Your adventurer meeple enters the leftmost space and moves right, space by space.
- Each dragon you pass through activates its ability in sequence.
- After the last dragon (or last space), gain the cavern's printed explore reward.
- **Full cavern (4 dragons)** — 4 ability activations plus the end-of-row reward. Massive value for a single action.

subtopics.title: Dragon Guild & Abilities
subtopics.content:
#### The Dragon Guild Track
- **Circular shared track** — Advance through exploration icons, card abilities, and other effects.
- **Cascading bonuses** — When your guild token moves, gain the bonus of every space you land on AND pass through.
- **Brown spaces** — When you land on one, place a player marker on the Dragon Guild tile to claim a benefit. You only get 8 markers total.
- **Guild tile slot priority:**
  1. 4th slot (end-game VP scoring) — most valuable, claim first
  2. 5th slot (end-game scoring) — second priority
  3. 3rd slot (extra action/coin) — immediate tempo boost
  4. 1st/2nd slots (discounted actions) — smallest benefit

#### Dragon Ability Types
- **When Played** — Triggers once, immediately when you place the dragon. A one-time bonus.
- **When Activated** — Triggers every time you explore that cavern. Engine-building core — gets more valuable with each exploration.
- **Once Per Round** — Triggers once each round, regardless of how often you explore.
- **End of Game** — Awards VP at final scoring based on conditions.

#### Hatchlings
- 35 of the 183 dragons are hatchlings.
- Require caching resources or tucking cards onto them over multiple turns.
- **Threshold** — Usually 3 items cached or tucked. When reached, the powerful third-line ability fires once.
- **High investment, high payoff** — Place early (Round 1-2) to have time to feed them. Round 4 hatchlings rarely reach threshold.

#### Dragon Personalities
- 4 types: Aggressive, Helpful, Playful, and Shy.
- Some dragons score bonus VP for being adjacent to matching personalities in the same cavern.

#### Key Rules to Remember
- **Coins are NOT resources** — Abilities that discount or convert resources do not apply to coins.
- **2-for-1 conversion** — Trade any 2 resource tokens for 1 resource of any type, at any point during your turn, as many times as needed.
- **Leftover conversion** — At end-game, every 4 leftover items (cards + resources) convert to 1 VP. Incomplete sets of 4 are worth nothing.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
After all players complete Round 4.

#### 8 VP Sources
1. **Dragon VP** — Point value printed on each placed dragon.
2. **Eggs** — 1 VP each for every egg remaining on your mat.
3. **Tucked cards** — 1 VP each for every card tucked under your dragons.
4. **Cached resources** — 1 VP each for resources cached on hatchling dragons.
5. **Round objectives** — 4 objective tiles score progressively; later rounds worth more VP.
6. **Dragon Guild tile markers** — VP from claimed guild tile slots, especially end-game scoring slots.
7. **End-game dragon abilities** — Some dragons award VP at final scoring based on conditions.
8. **Leftover conversion** — Every 4 leftover items (cards + resources) = 1 VP.

#### Tiebreaker
Most remaining resources. If still tied: shared victory.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Key Principles
- **Coins equal actions — spend them all.** 6 coins minimum each round = 6 actions. Unspent coins at round end are completely wasted.
- **Follow the excavate-entice-explore rhythm.** Excavate spaces, place dragons, then explore to activate all dragons and collect the row reward. Exploring empty caverns wastes actions.
- **Focus one cavern deep.** 4 dragons in one cavern = 4 ability activations plus explore reward per action. Spreading across 3 caverns gives far less value.
- **Start with the Crimson Cavern.** Resource-generating dragons here fuel everything else. If your opening hand has a cheap meat-cost dragon, Crimson is almost always your best first home.
- **The Dragon Guild matters more than you think.** Core resource and scoring engine — bonuses, resources, and end-game VP slots on the guild tile.
- **Eggs are precious.** 1 VP each at game end, but also needed as escalation costs. Every egg spent on costs is 1 VP you won't score.

#### Common Mistakes
- Exploring a cavern before placing any dragons in it — you get the row reward but miss all the activations
- Leaving unspent coins at round end — those are wasted actions you'll never get back
- Ignoring the Dragon Guild track — it's a major source of resources and end-game VP
- Spreading dragons evenly across all 3 caverns instead of going deep in one
- Not reading all 4 objective tiles during setup — you need to plan around them from turn 1

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### If You've Played Wingspan
Wyrmspan shares ~85% of its DNA with Wingspan, with key refinements:
- **No dice** — Resources from exploration are predictable. No birdfeeder luck.
- **Coins equal actions** — 6 per round (not the shrinking 8/7/6/5 of Wingspan). Bonus coins = extra actions.
- **Two-step placement** — Excavate a space (cave card), then place a dragon. Two separate actions instead of Wingspan's one.
- **Dragon Guild** — Circular shared track with cascading bonuses. No Wingspan equivalent.
- **Hatchlings** — 35 dragons requiring multi-turn investment before their big payoff.
- **Left-to-right exploration** — Wingspan activates birds right-to-left; Wyrmspan goes left-to-right.

#### Your First Game Setup
- 3 cavern rows, 4 slots each, column 1 pre-excavated in every row
- 3 dragon cards, 3 cave cards, 1 objective, 3 resources, 6 coins, 0 eggs

#### Round 1 (6 Coins = 6 Actions)
1. **Excavate** — Crimson Cavern column 2. Play a cave card, gain its bonus. 1 coin.
2. **Entice** — Place a dragon into Crimson column 1 (pre-excavated). Pay its resource cost. Resolve any "when played" ability. 1 coin.
3. **Entice** — Place another dragon into Crimson column 2 (just excavated). Pay its cost. 1 coin.
4. **Explore** — Crimson Cavern. Adventurer moves left to right — both dragons activate, then collect explore reward (resources + guild advancement). 1 coin.
5. **Remaining 2 coins** — Excavate or entice in another cavern, or explore Crimson again (2nd exploration costs 1 coin + 1 egg). Spend all coins!

#### Rounds 2-3: Building the Engine
- Fill your focus cavern to 4 dragons — maximizes exploration value.
- Advance on the Dragon Guild track whenever possible.
- Steer toward objective tiles worth the most points.

#### Round 4: Scoring Push
- Play high-VP dragons if available.
- Lay eggs for end-game points.
- Complete any hatchlings you've been feeding.
- Check your objective one last time.

#### First Game Target
- Aim for 70-85 VP. Focus on filling one cavern deep rather than spreading thin across all three.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Wingspan vs Wyrmspan: Strategic Differences
- **No dice** — Precise resource planning. You always know exactly what exploration yields.
- **Bonus coins** — The single most important strategic lever. 8 actions per round crushes 6.
- **Two-step placement** — Excavate then entice means slower engine development. Engine must be online by Round 2.
- **Guild track** — Major VP and resource source with no Wingspan equivalent. Ignoring it is a serious mistake.
- **Hatchlings** — Mini-quests requiring sustained investment over multiple rounds.

#### Engine Types
- **Crimson Focus (Safest)** — Fill Crimson Cavern with resource-generating dragons. Every exploration cascades into more resources. Most reliable approach.
- **Golden Focus (Card Engine)** — Tucking engine in Golden Grotto. Every tucked card = 1 VP. Golden dragons generate card flow for combos.
- **Amethyst Focus (Hatchlings)** — Self-sustaining cave card pipeline. Pair with hatchlings for highest ceiling, highest variance.
- **Recommended** — Go deep in 1 cavern (4 dragons by Round 3). Add a light second cavern (1-2 dragons) for support.

#### Guild Tile Priority
1. **4th slot (end-game scoring)** — Claim first. Highest-value slot.
2. **5th slot (end-game scoring)** — Second priority.
3. **3rd slot (extra action/coin)** — Tempo boost, pays off immediately.
4. **1st/2nd slots (discounted actions)** — Smallest benefit.
- At 4-5 players, race for brown spaces — guild tile gets contested hard and early claims matter.

#### Coin Optimization
- **Spend all 6+ coins every round** — Unspent coins are wasted turns.
- **Column 4 special ability** — Spend 3 items to gain 1 coin. Very powerful because each extra coin = extra action.
- **Seek coin-granting dragons and guild bonuses** — 7-8 actions per round gives a massive advantage over 6.

#### Hatchling Strategy
- **Place early** — Round 1 or 2, in the Amethyst Abyss where exploration feeds them.
- **Threshold** — Most need 3 cached items to trigger their powerful one-time ability.
- **Commit or skip** — Drafting a hatchling in Round 3-4 rarely gives enough time to reach threshold.

#### Round Objectives
- **Study all 4 during setup** — They shape your entire game plan.
- **Later rounds worth more VP** — Can be worth sacrificing Round 1-2 performance to dominate Rounds 3-4.
- **Avoid zeros** — Scoring 0 on any objective is an automatic 0 VP for that round. Even 1 point beats nothing.

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2-3 Players** — Dragon Guild less contested, more timing flexibility. Card display refreshes slowly — plan around what's available.
- **4-5 Players** — Guild competition fierce. Display churns faster. Watch opponents' drafting and adjust.

#### Key Decisions
1. **Setup** — Choose 3 starting resources that support both your opening dragons and your first guild advance.
2. **Round 1** — Establish the excavate-entice-excavate-entice-explore rhythm in your focus cavern.
3. **Round 2** — Commit to your focus cavern. Want 4 dragons in it by end of this round or early Round 3.
4. **Round 3** — Race to claim the 4th guild tile slot for end-game scoring.
5. **Round 4** — Stop building, start scoring. Every action should directly generate VP.

key_rulings.text: Coins represent actions, not resources. Abilities that gain, convert, or discount resources do not apply to coins. Coins cannot be traded using the 2-for-1 resource conversion.
You may only place a dragon in a space that has been excavated (has a cave card). Column 1 of each cavern is pre-excavated at game start; columns 2-4 require cave cards.
The first time you explore a cavern each round is free (1 coin only). The second exploration of the same cavern costs 1 coin + 1 egg. The third costs 1 coin + 2 eggs.
At the end of each round, any unspent coins are lost. They do not carry over to the next round. Spend all your coins each round to maximize your actions.
At the end of your turn, you may not have more than 9 total items in your personal supply (dragon cards + cave cards + resource tokens combined). Discard excess items of your choice.
At any point during your turn, you may exchange any 2 resource tokens for 1 resource token of any type. This conversion can be done multiple times per turn.

key_rulings.topic: Dragon placement — leftmost excavated empty slot
key_rulings.text: When enticing a dragon, you must place it in the leftmost excavated-but-empty slot of the matching cavern. You cannot skip spaces.

Hatchling dragons require you to cache resources or tuck cards onto them. When the number of cached/tucked items reaches the hatchling's threshold (shown on the card), the hatchling's powerful third-line ability triggers once.
When you excavate the 4th column of any cavern, you unlock a special ability: spend 3 items (any combination of resources, dragon cards, or cave cards) to gain 1 coin. This can be used once per turn.
At the end of the game, every 4 leftover items (cards in hand plus unspent resources) convert to 1 victory point. Remaining items that do not form a complete set of 4 are worth nothing.

key_rulings.topic: 'In this cave' means one row only
key_rulings.text: 'In this cave' refers to the specific row in which the dragon is located (Crimson Cavern, Golden Grotto, or Amethyst Abyss), NOT your entire player mat. Do not apply these abilities across all three rows.
Cached resources are not in your supply, and you are not able to spend them. Cached resources remain on the dragon card until end of game and are worth 1 VP each unless a card specifies otherwise.

key_rulings.topic: Any resource icon means ONE resource
key_rulings.text: When you see the any-resource icon, you gain any ONE resource of your choice (meat, milk, crystal, or gold). You do NOT gain one of each resource. Coins are not resources and cannot be selected.

key_rulings.topic: Free excavation waives egg costs too
key_rulings.text: When a card ability says you may 'excavate a cave for free,' this negates BOTH the coin cost of the Excavate action AND the egg cost associated with placing a cave card in columns 3 or 4.
